channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Tries to Downplay Economic Effects of the Iran War
item.description: At a White House event for Small Business Week, he described the economy as “roaring” and predicted that gas prices would go down soon.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking to small-business owners before Monday’s event, which the White House described as highlighting “the extraordinary revival of Main Street under his America First agenda.”

item.title: U.S. Warns China Over Iranian Oil as Sanctions Fight Intensifies
item.description: China told its independent refineries to disregard U.S. sanctions over their purchases of Iranian crude.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The warning came from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who is expected to participate in high-stakes meetings this month between the United States and China.

item.title: Attacks Threaten to Reignite Iran Conflict
item.description: Also, the stars arrive at the Met Gala. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz in April.

item.title: Trump Administration Demands Names of 2020 Election Workers in Georgia
item.description: The Justice Department again appears to be using the investigative power of the federal government to rehash debunked claims that Democrats stole the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti, Richard Fausset and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
item.media:description: F.B.I. agents raided Fulton County election offices in January, seizing boxes of ballots and other materials.

item.title: Soil at D.C. Golf Course Where East Wing Debris Was Dumped Contains Toxic Metals
item.description: The National Park Service said the dump of debris, between the fourth and ninth holes, does not exceed environmental limits. Opponents disagree.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow
item.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
item.media:description: Golfing in view of a dump site of debris and soil from the demolition of the White House’s East Wing at the East Potomac Golf Course in Washington in January.

item.title: White House Considers Vetting A.I. Models Before They Are Released
item.description: The Trump administration, which took a noninterventionist approach to artificial intelligence, is now discussing imposing oversight on A.I. models before they are made publicly available.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle, Julian E. Barnes, Sheera Frenkel and Dustin Volz
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Trump administration’s shift in policy began after Anthropic introduced a powerful artificial intelligence model called Mythos.

item.title: Met Gala 2026 Red Carpet Photos: All the Looks
item.description: See the arrivals on the cobblestone carpet.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk, Nina Westervelt and Amir Hamja
item.media:credit: Jamie Mccarthy/Getty Images

item.title: Supreme Court Temporarily Restores Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
item.description: A lower-court ruling had reinstated a Food and Drug Administration requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow and Pam Belluck
item.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Supreme Court has for now restored nationwide access to a widely used abortion pill.

item.title: Supreme Court Agrees to Fast-Track Louisiana Voting Map Decision
item.description: Louisiana voters who successfully challenged the state’s voting map as an illegal racial gerrymander had asked the justices to quickly return the case to the lower courts, clearing the way for a new map.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Supreme Court’s landmark opinion weakening the Voting Rights Act has set off a chaotic scramble in Louisiana ahead of a primary for House races.

item.title: New York Times Wins 3 Pulitzer Prizes
item.description: The Washington Post won the award for public service, considered the most prestigious of the Pulitzers, for its coverage of the Trump administration’s overhaul of federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: A photo that was part of the Pulitzer Prize-winning entry by Saher Alghorra, a contributor to The New York Times, in the breaking news photo category. It showed a child wounded in Gaza City being transferred to a hospital in April last year.

item.title: Pulitzer Prizes: 2026 Winners List
item.description: Here is the full list of winners and finalists.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: This image, showing displaced Palestinians walking to Gaza City in October 2025, was part of the entry by Saher Alghorra, a contributor to The New York Times who won for breaking news photography.

item.title: A Portfolio of Work From The Times’s Pulitzer Prize-Winning Photographer
item.description: The photographer Saher Alghorra reported on the Gaza conflict while enduring the dire conditions his images portrayed.
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Displaced Palestinians began returning to the north of Gaza after the Israeli military announced that a cease-fire was in effect.

item.title: Home on the Range No More: Trump Wants Bison Gone
item.description: The Trump administration is evicting bison herds from federal grasslands in Montana, siding with ranchers and Republican leaders over environmentalists and tribal leaders.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy and John Stember
item.media:description: American Prairie has identified the northern grasslands of Montana as the only place left in the United States to restore an entire prairie ecosystem.

item.title: More Than 150 Wind Projects Stall as Pentagon Delays Reviews
item.description: The delays, which companies say have worsened significantly in recent weeks, are the latest step in the Trump administration’s efforts to block wind power.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Scott Olson/Getty Images
item.media:description: Wind turbine components on a lot in Iowa last summer.

item.title: Chasing Stardom in Korea Nearly Destroyed Ejae. Then Came ‘KPop Demon Hunters.’
item.description: Ejae, the movie’s breakout star, was the face of K-pop in 2025. Facing questions of art, national belonging and authenticity, she wants to be much more.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim
item.media:credit: Woohae Cho for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ejae in the Hannam neighborhood of Seoul in April. She spent about a decade training to be a K-pop star in the city.

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni Settle ‘It Ends With Us’ Lawsuit
item.description: Ms. Lively had accused Mr. Baldoni of waging a smear campaign against her after she complained of harassment when making a film together. He had denied her accusation.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:credit: Associated Press
item.media:description: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni starred in the 2024 movie “It Ends With Us.”

item.title: How a Hantavirus Infection Feels: ‘You Just Can’t Get the Air In’
item.description: Doctors say that the virus, which is rare, can quickly progress from flulike symptoms to something far more serious.
item.dc:creator: Simar Bajaj and Nina Agrawal

item.title: What to Know About the Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
item.description: Three passengers of the MV Hondius died after showing symptoms of the rare disease. Health authorities confirmed one case and are investigating five others.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young and Claire Moses
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The cruise ship MV Hondius stationed off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on Sunday.

item.title: Man Accused of Starting Palisades Fire Admired Luigi Mangione, Prosecutors Say
item.description: The Los Angeles suspect was lonely and angry and felt “enslaved” by rich people, prosecutors say. After being romantically spurned, they say, he went into the mountains and lit a fire.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Palisades fire killed 12 people and destroyed thousands of homes in the seaside Los Angeles neighborhood of Pacific Palisades in January 2025.

item.title: Elon Musk’s Lawyers Ask OpenAI’s President Why He Is Worth $30 Billion
item.description: The legal team implied in a federal trial that Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president and co-founder, was driven by greed rather than building safe A.I.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI’s Greg Brockman entering federal court on Monday with his wife, Anna Brockman.

item.title: Streisand, LuPone, Holliday, Menzel: Broadway’s Biggest Voices
item.description: Lea Michele, Christine Ebersole, Jennifer Hudson, Kristin Chenoweth, Adam Lambert and 10 others reflect on their favorite belters.

item.title: A Movie About Women Who Love Their Work? Groundbreaking.
item.description: It turns out that some women do, in fact, dream of labor, at least the kind that comes with a sense of mastery, agency and glamour.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg

item.title: Speak, Yuppie
item.description: The young urban professionals of the 1980s changed our cities, our politics and our meritocratic work lives.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Gottlieb
item.category: Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP

item.title: 1,500 Beagles From Ridglan Farms in Wisconsin Are Getting New Homes
item.description: Last month, animal rights activists tried to steal some of the dogs from the Wisconsin facility, Ridglan Farms, but were thwarted by tear gas and rubber bullets.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin
item.media:credit: Taylor Glascock for The New York Times
item.media:description: Activists attempted to raid Ridglan Farms, near Madison, Wis., to rescue the beagles last month.

item.title: S.E.C. Settles Lawsuit Against Elon Musk Over His Twitter Disclosures
item.description: The agency, which has been pulling back on lawsuits against major companies, ended a case that had accused Mr. Musk of hiding his purchases of Twitter stock. He agreed to pay $1.5 million.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at federal court in San Francisco in March.

item.title: Four Epstein Victims Ask N.Y. Lawmakers to Open His Estate to Lawsuits
item.description: New York State law prevents people from seeking punitive damages from the estate of someone who has died.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes
item.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lara Blume McGee, center, and Carine Silva De Deus, left, were among four women who testified before the New York State Senate about being abused by Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Colorado Snowstorm Could Be Its Biggest All Year
item.description: Snow in May is not rare in the Rocky Mountains. But this year, any snow at all is unusual.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones
item.media:credit: The New York Times
